narration: Free shipping isn't actually free. The average online shopper spends nine hundred and seventy-eight dollars extra a year just to qualify for it. Here's the trap.
visual: A shipping box with a hidden price tag reading $978. Dollar signs float away from the box. Dark background, orange accent. Big stat reveals center screen.

narration: Every retailer sets a free shipping threshold — usually around thirty-five dollars. Your cart hits twenty-nine, and you start hunting for something to add. That's the trap.
visual: Shopping cart SVG with three items. A progress bar fills toward $35 threshold. A banner pops: 'Just $6 more for free shipping!' Then bar fills to 100%.

narration: Seventy percent of online shoppers add items just to hit that threshold. The average extra spend is fourteen dollars per order. Multiply that across every order all year.
visual: Two animated bar charts: 70% tall orange bar vs 30% gray bar. Below, a $14 stat card springs in from the left with a highlight border.

narration: Here's what makes it worse: forty-six percent of those filler items rarely ever get used. You paid fourteen extra dollars for something you basically threw away.
visual: Three product boxes drop into frame. Red X marks appear over two of them. A piggy bank sits below. A '46%' stat card reveals at the bottom.

narration: Retailers don't offer free shipping to be generous. It raises their average order value by thirty percent. You think you're winning the deal — they know you never were.
visual: Person silhouette on left, store building on right. Dollar sign icons fly from the person to the store. A '+30% higher order value' badge appears for the store.

narration: The fix is simple: next time you hit that threshold, ask yourself if you'd drive to a store for that item. If not, just pay the five-dollar fee. Follow for more money traps.
visual: Phone screen showing checkout with $4.99 standard shipping highlighted in green and the 'add $6 more' option crossed out in red. Savings bar fills to $978. CTA text springs in.
